description: Section-level EARS traceability from normative requirements to design, implementation, and test nodes

records.ears: The system shall provide the Compiler 2 product, installation path, and interactive environment defined by the product requirements.
When the Phase 1 product is installed, the system shall enter the resident editor through the production loader path.
The system shall implement the documented BASIC language profiles with stock-compatible semantics where compatibility is required.
When BASIC V2 syntax is executed, the system shall match the documented stock BASIC V2 behavior and compatibility limits.
Where a BASIC V3.5 extension is implemented, the system shall match the documented Plus/4 BASIC V3.5 behavior.
When a stock semantic result is asserted, the system shall derive it from an immutable versioned oracle fixture.
When input is entered in direct or program mode, the system shall dispatch it according to the documented mode contract.
When a tokenized program is loaded, stored, or saved, the system shall preserve the documented Commodore program format.
When a program is compiled and run, the system shall execute the documented compiler and runtime production pipeline.
When a stored program changes, the system shall incrementally rebuild the affected compiled state according to the generation contract.
When COMPILE exports a program, the system shall produce the documented standalone source-free artifact or report a defined error.
The system shall manage compiler and runtime memory through the documented arenas, handles, and lifetime rules.
When the production image is linked, the system shall conform to the nominal CPU memory map.
When graphics modes are used, the system shall preserve the documented graphics memory ownership and layout.
When an artifact is built, the system shall reject any hard memory budget overflow.
Where geoRAM or REU expansion memory is available, the system shall use the documented device profile without changing language semantics.
When expansion hardware is detected, the system shall apply the common dual-device selection, ownership, and fallback rules.
When expansion detection completes, the system shall publish one canonical expansion profile record.
When a DMA operation is requested, the system shall classify its CPU range before selecting a device transfer path.
When memory is copied through an expansion backend, the system shall obey the shared memcopy policy.
When expansion memory is filled, the system shall obey the shared memfill policy.
Where geoRAM is selected, the system shall access it through the guarded geoRAM backend contract.
Where REU is selected, the system shall execute page-buffer overlays through the documented REU backend.
If expansion assistance degrades or changes, the system shall re-detect safely and retain the defined minimal editor behavior.
When the editor and runtime handle interrupts, the system shall preserve machine state and meet the documented responsiveness contract.
When the compiler invokes KERNAL services, the system shall preserve the documented ABI, device state, and banking rules.
When optimization is enabled, the system shall preserve observable program semantics and obey the documented optimization limits.
If a recoverable or fatal error occurs, the system shall expose deterministic diagnostics and preserve the documented recovery behavior.
When the project is built, the system shall use the pinned toolchain and emit validated reproducible contracts and artifacts.
When behavior is verified, the system shall use the documented unit, integration, functional, system, E2E, and hardware test hierarchy.
When a critical language behavior is implemented, the system shall cover it in the required BASIC profile and execution modes.
When stock semantics are asserted, the system shall retain versioned reference inputs, outputs, ROM checksums, and normalization metadata.
When requirements or their verification change, the system shall regenerate and validate complete bidirectional traceability artifacts.
Where the REU product profile is built, the system shall preserve the documented Compiler 2 compatibility contract.
When REU hardware is detected, the system shall report and use only supported capacity and device capabilities.
When the REU performs DMA, the system shall program the REC and transfer ranges according to the documented contract.
When installing REU support, the system shall detect the device without destructively modifying user-visible memory.
When an REU overlay is invoked, the system shall execute it from normal RAM through the documented overlay boundary.
When data moves between CPU and REU memory, the system shall preserve arena handle and transfer invariants.
When strings or cached data reside in REU memory, the system shall preserve their documented lifetime and access semantics.
When REU operations interact with CPU mapping or interrupts, the system shall preserve responsiveness and machine state.
When the REU edition is packaged, the system shall emit a loader and images that match the generated layout contracts.
When REU contracts are generated, the system shall validate their addresses, sizes, versions, and checksums against the built artifacts.
If REU detection or transfer fails, the system shall report deterministic diagnostics and recover according to the documented policy.
When REU support is accepted, the system shall pass the required unit, integration, system, and hardware verification layers.
When the REU edition reaches its acceptance gate, the system shall satisfy every recorded requirement and verification obligation.
